children.heading: Priority review
children.label: 1 Priority review
children.text: The term “priority review”, with respect to a human drug application as defined in , means review and action by the Secretary on such application not later than 6 months after receipt by the Secretary of such application, as described in the Manual of Policies and Procedures of the Food and Drug Administration and goals identified in the letters described in section 101(b) of the Prescription Drug User Fee Amendments of 2012.section 379g(1) of this title

children.heading: Priority review voucher
children.label: 2 Priority review voucher
children.text: The term “priority review voucher” means a voucher issued by the Secretary to the sponsor of a rare pediatric disease product application that entitles the holder of such voucher to priority review of a single human drug application submitted under or section 351(a) of the Public Health Service Act [] after the date of approval of the rare pediatric disease product application.section 355(b)(1) of this title42 U.S.C. 262(a)

children.label: A
children.text: The disease is a serious or life-threatening disease in which the serious or life-threatening manifestations primarily affect individuals aged from birth to 18 years, including age groups often called neonates, infants, children, and adolescents.

children.label: B
children.text: The disease is a rare disease or condition, within the meaning of .section 360bb of this title

children.label: A
children.text: is for a drug or biological product that is for the prevention or treatment of a rare pediatric disease;

children.label: I
children.text: that contains no active moiety (as defined by the Secretary in section 314.3 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulations)) that has been previously approved in any other application under subsection (b)(1), (b)(2), or (j) of ; andsection 355 of this title

children.label: II
children.text: that is the subject of an application submitted under ; orsection 355(b)(1) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: that contains no active ingredient that has been previously approved in any other application under section 351(a) or 351(k) of the Public Health Service Act [, 262(k)]; and42 U.S.C. 262(a)

children.label: II
children.text: that is the subject of an application submitted under section 351(a) of the Public Health Service Act [];42 U.S.C. 262(a)

children.label: C
children.text: the Secretary deems eligible for priority review;

children.label: D
children.text: that relies on clinical data derived from studies examining a pediatric population and dosages of the drug intended for that population;1So in original. The word “that” probably should not appear.1

children.label: E
children.text: that does not seek approval for an adult indication in the original rare pediatric disease product application; and1

children.label: F
children.text: is approved after .September 30, 2016

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall award a priority review voucher to the sponsor of a rare pediatric disease product application upon approval by the Secretary of such rare pediatric disease product application.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The sponsor of a rare pediatric disease product application that receives a priority review voucher under this section may transfer (including by sale) the entitlement to such voucher. There is no limit on the number of times a priority review voucher may be transferred before such voucher is used.

children.heading: Notification of transfer
children.label: B Notification of transfer
children.text: Each person to whom a voucher is transferred shall notify the Secretary of such change in ownership of the voucher not later than 30 days after such transfer.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 3 Limitation
children.text: A sponsor of a rare pediatric disease product application may not receive a priority review voucher under this section if the rare pediatric disease product application was submitted to the Secretary prior to the date that is 90 days after .July 9, 2012

children.heading: Sponsor of a rare pediatric disease product
children.label: A Sponsor of a rare pediatric disease product

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Beginning on the date that is 90 days after , the sponsor of a rare pediatric disease product application that intends to request a priority review voucher under this section shall notify the Secretary of such intent upon submission of the rare pediatric disease product application that is the basis of the request for a priority review voucher.September 30, 2016

children.heading: Applications submitted but not yet approved
children.label: ii Applications submitted but not yet approved
aa
children.text: on or after the date that is 90 days after ; andJuly 9, 2012

children.label: bb
children.text: on or before ; andSeptember 30, 2016

children.label: II
children.text: such application otherwise meets the criteria for a priority review voucher under this section.

children.heading: Sponsor of a drug application using a priority review voucher
children.label: B Sponsor of a drug application using a priority review voucher

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The sponsor of a human drug application shall notify the Secretary not later than 90 days prior to submission of the human drug application that is the subject of a priority review voucher of an intent to submit the human drug application, including the date on which the sponsor intends to submit the application. Such notification shall be a legally binding commitment to pay the user fee to be assessed in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Transfer after notice
children.label: ii Transfer after notice
children.text: The sponsor of a human drug application that provides notification of the intent of such sponsor to use the voucher for the human drug application under clause (i) may transfer the voucher after such notification is provided, if such sponsor has not yet submitted the human drug application described in the notification.

children.heading: Termination of authority
children.label: 5 Termination of authority
children.text: The Secretary may not award any priority review vouchers under paragraph (1) after .September 30, 2029

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a user fee program under which a sponsor of a human drug application that is the subject of a priority review voucher shall pay to the Secretary a fee determined under paragraph (2). Such fee shall be in addition to any fee required to be submitted by the sponsor under subchapter VII.

children.label: A
children.text: the average cost incurred by the Food and Drug Administration in the review of a human drug application subject to priority review in the previous fiscal year; and

children.label: B
children.text: the average cost incurred by the Food and Drug Administration in the review of a human drug application that is not subject to priority review in the previous fiscal year.

children.heading: Annual fee setting
children.label: 3 Annual fee setting
children.text: The Secretary shall establish, before the beginning of each fiscal year beginning after , the amount of the priority review user fee for that fiscal year.September 30, 2012

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The priority review user fee required by this subsection shall be due upon the submission of a human drug application under or section 351(a) of the Public Health Service Act [] for which the priority review voucher is used. All other user fees associated with the human drug application shall be due as required by the Secretary or under applicable law.section 355(b)(1) of this title42 U.S.C. 262(a)

children.heading: Complete application
children.label: B Complete application
children.text: An application described under subparagraph (A) for which the sponsor requests the use of a priority review voucher shall be considered incomplete if the fee required by this subsection and all other applicable user fees are not paid in accordance with the Secretary’s procedures for paying such fees.

children.heading: No waivers, exemptions, reductions, or refunds
children.label: C No waivers, exemptions, reductions, or refunds
children.text: The Secretary may not grant a waiver, exemption, reduction, or refund of any fees due and payable under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be deposited and credited as offsetting collections to the account providing appropriations to the Food and Drug Administration; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall not be collected for any fiscal year except to the extent provided in advance in appropriations Acts.

children.label: A
children.text: the new drug as a drug for a rare pediatric disease; and

children.label: B
children.text: the application for the new drug as a rare pediatric disease product application.

children.heading: Request for designation
children.label: 2 Request for designation
children.text: The request for a designation under paragraph (1) shall be made at the same time a request for designation of orphan disease status under or fast-track designation under is made. Requesting designation under this subsection is not a prerequisite to receiving a priority review voucher under this section.section 360bb of this titlesection 356 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the disease or condition that is the subject of such request is a rare pediatric disease; and

children.label: B
children.text: the application for the new drug is a rare pediatric disease product application.

children.heading: Marketing of rare pediatric disease products
children.label: e Marketing of rare pediatric disease products

children.heading: Revocation
children.label: 1 Revocation
children.text: The Secretary may revoke any priority review voucher awarded under subsection (b) if the rare pediatric disease product for which such voucher was awarded is not marketed in the United States within the 365-day period beginning on the date of the approval of such drug under or section 351 of the Public Health Service Act [].section 355 of this title42 U.S.C. 262

children.label: A
children.text: The estimated population in the United States suffering from the rare pediatric disease.

children.label: B
children.text: The estimated demand in the United States for such rare pediatric disease product.

children.label: C
children.text: The actual amount of such rare pediatric disease product distributed in the United States.

children.heading: Notice of issuance of voucher and approval of products under voucher
children.label: 1 Notice of issuance of voucher and approval of products under voucher
A
children.text: The Secretary issues a priority review voucher under this section.

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary approves a drug pursuant to an application submitted under or section 351(a) of the Public Health Service Act [] for which the sponsor of the application used a priority review voucher under this section.section 355(b) of this title42 U.S.C. 262(a)

children.label: A
children.text: notifying such Committees of the use of such voucher; and

children.label: B
children.text: identifying the drug for which such priority review voucher is used.

children.heading: Eligibility for other programs
children.label: g Eligibility for other programs
children.text: Nothing in this section precludes a sponsor who seeks a priority review voucher under this section from participating in any other incentive program, including under this chapter, except that no sponsor of a rare pediatric disease product application may receive more than one priority review voucher issued under any section of this chapter with respect to the drug for which the application is made..3So in original.3

children.heading: Relation to other provisions
children.label: h Relation to other provisions
children.text: The provisions of this section shall supplement, not supplant, any other provisions of this chapter or the Public Health Service Act [ et seq.] that encourage the development of drugs for tropical diseases and rare pediatric diseases.42 U.S.C. 201

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Beginning on the date that the Secretary awards the third rare pediatric disease priority voucher under this section, the Comptroller General of the United States shall conduct a study of the effectiveness of awarding rare pediatric disease priority vouchers under this section in the development of human drug products that treat or prevent such diseases.

children.label: i
children.text: The indications for which each rare disease product for which a priority review voucher was awarded was approved under or section 351 of the Public Health Service Act [].section 355 of this title42 U.S.C. 262

children.label: ii
children.text: Whether, and to what extent, an unmet need related to the treatment or prevention of a rare pediatric disease was met through the approval of such a rare disease product.

children.label: iii
children.text: The value of the priority review voucher if transferred.

children.label: iv
children.text: Identification of each drug for which a priority review voucher was used.

children.label: v
children.text: The length of the period of time between the date on which a priority review voucher was awarded and the date on which it was used.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date under paragraph (1)(A), the Comptroller General shall submit to the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate, a report containing the results of the study under paragraph (1).
